ddls:
CREATE TABLE "test_runs" (
  "run_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test run */,
  "run_number" INTEGER /* Sequential number of the test run */,
  "test_scenario_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test scenario being performed */,
  "software_version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the software version used in the test run */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test environment */,
  "vehicle_config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the vehicle configuration used in the test run */,
  "success_rate" TEXT /* Success rate of the test run, including improvement (I) if applicable */,
  "distance_traveled" REAL /* Distance traveled during the test run */,
  "time_taken" TEXT /* Time taken to complete the test run */,
  "distance_left" REAL /* Distance left to complete the test run */,
  "run_date" TEXT /* Date when the test run was performed */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any notes or observations about the test run */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the test run */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the test run */,
  "test_run_status" TEXT /* Status of the test run (e.g., completed, aborted, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("run_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_environment_id FOREIGN KEY ("environment_id") REFERENCES test_environments ("environment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_vehicle_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("vehicle_config_id") REFERENCES vehicle_configurations ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_software_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("software_version_id") REFERENCES software_versions ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_runs_test_scenario_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_scenario_id") REFERENCES test_scenarios ("scenario_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_scenarios" (
  "scenario_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test scenario */,
  "scenario_name" TEXT /* Name of the test scenario */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the test scenario */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test environment */,
  "route_map" TEXT /* Route map for the test scenario */,
  "obstacles" TEXT /* List of obstacles in the test scenario */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions for the test scenario */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scenario_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_scenarios_environment_id FOREIGN KEY ("environment_id") REFERENCES test_environments ("environment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "software_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each software version */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the software */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date of the software release */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any notes or release information about the software version */,
  "change_log" TEXT /* Change log for the software version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_environments" (
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test environment */,
  "environment_name" TEXT /* Name of the test environment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the test environment */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the test environment */,
  "environment_type" TEXT /* Type of the test environment (e.g., indoor, outdoor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("environment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicle_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle configuration */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the vehicle configuration */,
  "vehicle_type" TEXT /* Type of the vehicle (e.g., SUV, Sedan) */,
  "sensor_setup" TEXT /* Detailed setup of sensors and other equipment */,
  "software_version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the software version used in the configuration */,
  "hardware_version" TEXT /* Version of the vehicle hardware */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vehicle_configurations_software_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("software_version_id") REFERENCES software_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_run_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "run_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test run the log belongs to */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of the log entry (e.g., sensor data, event) */,
  "log_data" TEXT /* Detailed data or description of the log entry */,
  "log_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the log entry */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log entry (e.g., debug, info, error) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_run_logs_run_id FOREIGN KEY ("run_id") REFERENCES test_runs ("run_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, project manager, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., engineering, management) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "run_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test run being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the test run data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the test run data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location of the user when accessing the test run data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_run_id FOREIGN KEY ("run_id") REFERENCES test_runs ("run_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "run_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test run where the anomaly was observed */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of the anomaly (e.g., software bug, hardware failure) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the anomaly */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the anomaly resolution (e.g., open, resolved) */,
  "reported_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reported the anomaly */,
  "reported_date" TEXT /* Date when the anomaly was reported */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority of the anomaly (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_reported_by FOREIGN KEY ("reported_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_run_id FOREIGN KEY ("run_id") REFERENCES test_runs ("run_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vehicle_health" (
  "health_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vehicle health record */,
  "vehicle_config_id" INTEGER /* ID of the vehicle configuration */,
  "health_status" TEXT /* Status of the vehicle health (e.g., good, bad) */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "repair_date" TEXT /* Date of the last repair */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any notes or comments about the vehicle health */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("health_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vehicle_health_vehicle_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("vehicle_config_id") REFERENCES vehicle_configurations ("config_id")
)
